label: Full Body 2-day
summary: Two broad full-body sessions with simple movement coverage and manageable weekly recovery.
when_to_prefer: best when only two sessions are available
easy to keep balanced
when_to_avoid: less ideal for people wanting a lot of specialization

label: Upper/Lower 2-day
summary: One upper session and one lower session for lifters who prefer simpler focus per day.
when_to_prefer: good when sessions are long enough for concentrated work
when_to_avoid: less balanced if sessions need to stay very short

label: Full Body 3-day
summary: Three balanced full-body sessions with slightly different emphasis across the week.
when_to_prefer: strong default for general fitness
easy to keep balanced across three days
when_to_avoid: can feel repetitive for athletes wanting strong specialization

label: Push/Pull/Legs 3-day
summary: Three specialized days with clearer body-part separation and higher per-session concentration.
when_to_prefer: good for athletes who like focused days
fits longer sessions well
when_to_avoid: less ideal for very short sessions or beginners needing more frequency per muscle

label: Upper/Lower/Full Body 3-day
summary: A hybrid split that gives two focused days plus one broad catch-all session.
when_to_prefer: useful when you want balance plus some variety
when_to_avoid: less simple than a plain full-body plan

label: Upper/Lower 4-day
summary: A classic four-day structure with enough room for balanced volume and varied emphasis.
when_to_prefer: strong default for four sessions per week
supports balanced hypertrophy-style training
when_to_avoid: less ideal if recovery is poor or sessions must be very short

label: Full Body 4-day
summary: Frequent full-body training with smaller per-session workloads and repeated movement practice.
when_to_prefer: helpful when shorter sessions are spread across more days
when_to_avoid: can feel too repetitive for athletes wanting specialization

label: Body-Part 5-day
summary: Five specialized sessions with high variety and strong per-session focus.
when_to_prefer: best for athletes wanting focused body-part days
when_to_avoid: not ideal for beginners or limited session time

label: Upper/Lower/Push/Pull/Legs 5-day
summary: A higher-frequency five-day hybrid with both broad and specialized sessions.
when_to_prefer: good for lifters wanting five days without repeating the same focus
when_to_avoid: can be too much for poor recovery or very short sessions

label: Push/Pull/Legs/Upper/Lower 5-day
summary: A five-day hybrid that starts with a push-pull-legs rotation and finishes with balanced upper-lower sessions.
when_to_prefer: good for lifters who want a PPL feel with extra balanced upper and lower work
useful when a user explicitly asks for PPL plus upper-lower structure
when_to_avoid: can be too dense for poor recovery or inconsistent attendance

label: Push/Pull/Legs 6-day
summary: A classic six-day push-pull-legs rotation run twice per week for high frequency and specialization.
when_to_prefer: good for athletes who recover well and want repeated muscle exposure
fits lifters who like specialized training days
when_to_avoid: not ideal for beginners, poor recovery, or very short sessions

label: Upper/Lower 6-day
summary: An alternating upper-lower schedule across six days for lifters who want frequent but simple day structure.
when_to_prefer: useful when you want six days but still prefer a simple repeating structure
works well for balanced lifters who like predictability
when_to_avoid: can feel repetitive if the athlete wants more variety

label: Arnold + PPL 6-day
summary: A six-day hybrid that blends chest-back and shoulder-arm specialization with a dedicated legs pattern.
when_to_prefer: good for advanced lifters who want high variety and body-part emphasis
when_to_avoid: not ideal for simple plans or limited recovery

label: Body-Part 7-day
summary: A full seven-day body-part split with one main emphasis each day and very high specialization.
when_to_prefer: best for athletes who explicitly want daily specialization
works when recovery and schedule are both excellent
when_to_avoid: not ideal for general fitness, beginners, or limited equipment

label: PPL + Upper/Lower 7-day
summary: A seven-day hybrid that combines a push-pull-legs rotation with extra upper-lower balance work.
when_to_prefer: good for high-frequency lifters who still want balanced weekly coverage
when_to_avoid: too dense for athletes with poor recovery or inconsistent attendance

label: Full Body 7-day Microcycle
summary: Seven shorter full-body sessions with rotating emphasis for athletes who prefer daily training without hard body-part splits.
when_to_prefer: useful when daily training is desired but sessions stay short and manageable
when_to_avoid: not ideal if the athlete prefers highly specialized days
